Liz 2 zipped up the turquoise dress Carly was now wearing. Carly turned around to face her. "Thanks honey, you sure do make life easier around here." Carly said. She was rewarded with a smile. The smile turned into a frown as she heard Carly's door buzzer. "It's okay. We'll get through this. She might be angry but she's still my friend and I know she's not a monster."

Carly picked up the intercom handset. Liz's voice came through it. "Hi Carly, it's me. I'm here to collect, ...her." Carly pressed the button that opened the security door. Both them waited nervously, until there was a knock on the flat door.

Carly walked over and opened it. "Hi Liz!" She said cheerily. Her new feelings for Liz 2 briefly made her feel like kissing Liz before she stopped herself. "Come in."

Liz had enough sense to be aware of the fact she'd been far too angry when she left her house. Fearing she'd do something stupid, she had stopped at a small park on the way here. She had sat on a bench calming down and collecting her thoughts for some time before resuming her drive here. She was still a little annoyed but not as angry as she had been. Carly and Liz 2 didn't know that though and assumed the worse.

Liz walked in and saw Liz 2 standing behind Carly wearing her work clothes from yesterday. "Okay, come on you." The relatively calm Liz, gestured with her arm.

Liz could see the looks Carly was giving her. She had expected Carly to be confused. "Look Carly, I know I owe you an explanation but..."

Carly smiled. "No need," Carly said as she pointed at Liz 2, "she's explained everything."

"I didn't think she was allowed..., I mean oh, okay." Liz quickly corrected herself.

"Yes, I think we should talk about what she is allowed and not allowed to do." Her expression was more serious now. It quickly changed back to a smile. "Let's have a cuppa and discuss it."

"Well...," Liz said reluctantly.

---

Liz 2 sat on the sofa and Liz sat across from her in one of the armchairs. As soon as Carly left to boil the kettle in the kitchen, Liz leaned over to Liz 2 and whispered, "What the chuffing hell do you think you are playing at?"

Liz 2 stared at her. "Yesterday, you said 'I'm sure with your talents, you'll easily find someone to have sex with'." Liz 2 copied Liz's angry tone from the previous day when she was quoting her. "You also said 'You'll be able to stay with them tonight'." When she finished, her face returned to a neutral expression.

Liz couldn't deny it. She tried extending an olive branch. "Okay, I was angry, I didn't really mean it, I'm sorry. It's just Carly is one of my best friends."

Liz 2's eyes narrowed. "Do I understand this correctly? Yesterday as I made a genuine attempt to offer an apology, when I confessed to making a terrible mistake, you got angry and threw me out of the house. Today you offer me an apology for a mistake you made, you wish me to be sympathetic?"

Liz 2 had made Liz's hypocrisy blatantly obvious. "Yes." She said quietly.

Liz 2's directives made it difficult to say what she wanted to say, so she had to reconstruct the point somewhat. "If our positions were reversed, you would tell me to piss off!" Before Liz could react, Carly came walking through from the kitchen carrying a tray.

"Now, now ladies, I hope you aren't arguing." She said as she sat down beside Liz 2. She put the tray on the coffee table. "From what I can see, you two have quite a lot to sort out. I want to be a mediator, a voice of reason, I'll try to be impartial and fair." She unconsciously placed her hand on Liz 2's thigh. It didn't go unnoticed by Liz.

"Ha!" She laughed derisively.

"You have a point to make Liz?" Carly asked her.

"You can't be fair, she's been shagging you all night." Liz said as her anger increased again as she addressed the elephant in the room. "I didn't even think you felt that way about women."

"Neither did I, she's made me understand myself better." She looked into Liz 2's eyes lovingly. Liz found it embarrassing. Carly quickly snapped out of it. She looked at Liz. "And you are one of my closest friends." Carly nodded. "You're right but she needs an advocate."

"She's quite capable of defending herself." Liz said.

"No she isn't. She's only existed a few days, she's relying on your way of thinking. She's her own person and she hasn't come to terms with that yet. I don't think either of you have. She's also got a head full of crazy thoughts, which is largely down to you."

"Me? What did I do?"

"Those stupid directive things, they should be the first to go." Carly told her. Liz realised exactly how much Liz 2 had told her.

"I need those to keep her in check." Liz explained.

"You were just about ready to kill her earlier, so we can see those work really well." Carly said sarcastically. She knew she was pushing Liz's buttons and tried to calm herself down. "You could try something new."

"Like what?" Liz asked.

"Trusting her. Actually ask her to be more considerate. Let her choose how to be, rather than force it on her."

Liz shook her head. "I tried trusting her and she shoved me into a cupboard. She pretended to be me for the rest of the night." Carly could barely stifle the grin. "I was in a stupid outfit too, again because I trusted her. Did she tell you that?"

Carly shook her head and covered her mouth. "No." She managed to say.

"It wasn't funny Carly." Carly attempted to compose herself. "It was chuffin' freezing." Liz shivered a little as she recalled it. She could see Carly smiling again. Liz felt compelled to smile herself. "Okay maybe it was a little bit funny." She admitted.

Carly turned to Liz 2. "Why?"

"Both of us were standing in the hall when the family returned from an outing. I was wearing my normal clothes and I thought hiding Liz was the best course of action to prevent discovery of my existence." Liz 2 explained.

"If Liz gave you a directive to keep yourself secret, she deserved everything she got." Carly said.

"I didn't do that." Liz said angrily. "I mean, yeah I didn't want everyone to know about her but I didn't expressly say anything about keeping it a secret."

"Liz is correct. It's a default manufacturer's directive. I have many." Liz 2 said.

Carly looked amazed. "So on top of Liz's directives there are more you are given when you are created?" Liz 2 nodded.

"Due to customer complaints more and more default directives have been added." Liz 2 explained.

Carly looked deep in thought. "Can we remove those too?" She asked.

"Liz can, yes. My manufacturers don't advise it though. It may cause undesirable results."

"What? You'll behave more like a human and less like a machine?" Carly asked as she turned to Liz "Let's do it."

"You sure about this Carly?" Liz asked. "It seems like those directives are there for a reason. I mean what if she loses all her morals, she could turn into a murderer or something."

Carly hadn't thought about that. Liz 2 spoke. "If you want to go ahead with this course of action, I'll remind you that I already do have moral and ethical framework based on Liz's."

Carly laughed. "I'm not sure if that's better or worse than having none at all."

"Hey!" Liz scowled at her friend. She picked up a cushion and threw it at Carly. It missed and bounced off the floor.

Carly turned to Liz 2. "I hope you can throw better than that." She said.

"Considerably." Liz 2 said without a hint of boasting. "If you are concerned, I suggest you get Liz to make my directives advisory."

Carly turned back to Liz again. "Well?"

"I guess we are doing this but if she starts chasing you with a meat cleaver, on your own head be it Carly." Liz warned. She turned to Liz 2. "Liz 2 go under!" She said.

"Is that what you call her?" Carly asked. Liz nodded.

"Yeah, it's what her manufacturer's called her, we really should come up with something better."

"Understood." Liz 2 said in a mechanical fashion.

"Oops, I've got to be careful when I talk to her like this." Liz took a breath. "All current directives are now to be treated as advisory only. You don't have to obey them from now on." There was a small delay.

"If you proceed with this action you will invalidate your terms and conditions agreement contract with the construct-o-matic corporation. Do you understand?" Liz 2 asked.

"Yes." Liz told her.

"This will also invalidate your warranty on this construct. Do you wish to proceed?"

"Yes." Liz said a little exasperated. Some things never change, she thought.

"Confirmation required, do you wish to proceed?"

"Yes!"

Silence followed. Carly looked at Liz 2 and then at Liz. "Has it worked?"

"One way to find out." Liz said as she turned back to Liz 2. "Wake up!"

Emma looked at the mystery woman carefully. She looked about forty and had brown hair that ended just above her shoulders. She had quite an attractive face with a cute little button nose. The large glasses she wore kept sliding down the cute little nose and she had to keep fixing their position. There was something very familiar about her face, Emma thought.

Looking further down, Emma could see the woman's breasts pressing against the oversized shirt she had on. It was quite clear she wasn't wearing a bra underneath. She was wearing trousers that looked too big for her too, her belt was cinching her waist.

The remote looked familiar too. Emma remembered where she had seen it before. On her desk. "Oh my God! Steven?" She asked.

"Yes, who else would it be?" Steven said as he looked up at the taller woman.

"You been playing with the stuff on my desk?" Emma asked pretending to sound a little annoyed.

"Sorry, I finished all the e-mails and I got bored. I got curious about all the things on your desk and I picked this up." He waved the remote control in the air. "I pushed a button and turned into this." He pointed at himself with his other hand.

"All those items on my desk, are magic or have powers."

Steven nodded. "I guessed it was something like that after I changed into a woman. I didn't dare touch anything else."

"I've been trying to get rid of them all. You can keep that if you want." Emma told him as she pointed at the remote.

"A lot of good it's done me. I kept pressing the button but nothing happened." He told her.

"Well it is a remote control luv. Did you try pointing it at yourself?" Emma asked. Steven nodded, feeling a little embarrassed.

"Eventually I did." Emma was aware Steven sometimes wasn't the sharpest person in the building.

"Give me a look at it." Emma said. Steven handed it to her. Most of the buttons appeared to have labels next to them.

Steven pointed at one button. "That's the one I pressed. It has some weird symbol on it." Emma looked at it. The symbol scrawled on the label was clearly the Venus symbol which Emma, like most people, also knew represented females. It wasn't a toggle between male and female, as Steven had assumed. It had no effect after turning the subject into a female, she established quickly.

Examining the other buttons she soon found the button label marked with the Mars symbol. "You don't know what these symbols mean?" Emma asked.

"No idea." Steven said. Knowing Steven, Emma wasn't surprised. She was about to point the remote at him and press the button marked with the male symbol when she changed her mind. She realised this was too good an opportunity to miss and decided to have a little fun with him.

"Yes, it's quite the mystery." She said as she continued to examine the device. Like her own device and Liam-Ruby's gun there was a reset button. It was actually marked 'RESET' rather than the cryptic 'R' on her own remote. She felt confident she could operate the device without worrying about permanently changing him. "Maybe we should try some of the other buttons." She suggested.

"If you think it will help." Steven said, somewhat nervously.

"Oh, I'm sure you'll see a difference." Emma said as she pointed the remote at him.

Liz 2 felt like her head exploded as billions of neurons in her brain reorganised themselves. She put both hands on her head as the pain increased. Closing her eyes she fell backwards on to the sofa. Everything went dark.

"Oh, God, I hope she's all right." Carly said as she leaned over her new lover. The worry was evident in her voice as Liz 2 twitched a little. Liz 2's breathing was laboured too.

Liz leaned over to look more closely at her duplicate. "It's like she's having a seizure." She was a little worried too.

Steven did use them, but he guessed in this new version of reality, things were different. "So you spend your time, watching other guys in the men's toilets?" Steven asked.

Aaron clearly didn't like the implication. "I don't and err...," he was struggling to find a good comeback after being bested by Steven, "if you like using the stalls so much, why don't you use the women's toilets, there are plenty in there." He grinned after managing to get a clever dig in at the supervisor.

"You seem to know a lot about the women's toilets. Sounds like, you've been peeking in there. I might have to report this to Emma." Steven warned him in a condescending fashion.

Aaron had ran out of witty insults. The threat of getting Emma involved had thrown him off kilter. "I, I, need to get back to work." He said.

"Good, don't let me stop you." Steven put his fists on his hips.

Aaron turned and disappeared quickly down the corridor. Steven let out his breath slowly. He couldn't believe he'd stood up to Aaron. Steven hated conflict normally. He didn't understand why but he felt so charged and full of spunk in this new body. He was enjoying the feeling a great deal. Maybe this wouldn't be so bad after all he thought.
1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.title: Time passes at the shop

choices: Carly finishes work and goes home
The end of the Saturday shift approaches
Mike and Natalies start their preparations
1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Steven sat alone in the break room as he enjoyed his very late lunch. He had prepared a packed meal which he retrieved from the fridge when he arrived in the room.

As he sat and ate, he was again somewhat disorientated by how big everything seemed now. He had prepared four sandwiches and so far he had struggled to eat the second one. Placing his hands on his belly, he could feel his tiny little stomach bulging as it tried to digest the relatively big sandwich.

He was annoyed by the idea of having to throw away the extra sandwiches. As he continued to think about it though he realised he could turn this to his advantage. One of his biggest expenditures was food. I'm going to save a fortune, he thought. He felt that this was another silver lining to having such a small body.

---

Steven got a few interesting looks as he got on to the shop floor. The staff seemed to be surprised to see him in clothes that actually fit him. He could see the shop was busy but everything seem to be running smoothly. The customers were moving through the tills calmly and quietly.

He noticed plenty of shelf-stackers working away efficiently. There didn't seem to be much of the excessive staff chatter and idleness that often plagued his shifts. Spotting Kelly behind the customer service desk, he walked over to her. The desk was quite high and nearly came up to his shoulders.

Waiting till she had finished with her current customer, he spoke. "How's it going?" He asked, his chirpy feminine tone briefly surprising him.

"Well, we're busy but no major disasters so far. I could do with a ten minute break though." Kelly said.

"Sorry, Emma told me to go get my lunch or I would have been down earlier." He said as he walked behind the desk.

"That conference call must have really dragged on to tie you up for so long."

"Yeah," he said feeling a little guilty for Emma's lies, "all sorted now."

"Oh, I've left some of the paperwork for you. I'm not a hundred percent sure how to complete it." She pulled out the supervisor's folder from under the desk. "I've been kind of too busy to do it anyway."

"That's fine. I'll handle it. Go get your ten minute." He told her.

"Thanks." Kelly said as she left the desk.

Steven grabbed a pen. He noticed as he opened the folder the desk height would make writing impractical. Looking around under the desk he found a foot stool stored there. The stool allowed the 'shortest' members of staff to use the desk comfortably. He sighed as he pulled it out and stood on it.

---

Kelly sneaked her way into the warehouse. She had tried to time it so all the stackers weren't near the end of their cages. She spotted Claire, who was being run ragged filling the cages for the stackers.

"Hi." Kelly said in a way that was a little too friendly for a work colleague.

"Hi." Claire said as she continued to work.

"How are you?" Kelly automatically moved into chit-chat mode.

"Busy and tired." She said as she closed a cage and grabbed an empty one. "I don't know why there is only one person filling cages on a Saturday. It's the busiest day of the week. How come nearly half of the staff get the day off?" Claire grumbled.

Kelly shrugged. "It's the weekend?" She said somewhat rhetorically.

"This company is stuck in the past. We're open seven days a week, have been for decades, the rotas should reflect that. Evenly distribute the days off."

Kelly smiled. She knew in a few years, Claire would make a good manager herself. She was strong, intelligent and dedicated. Those were also the kind of qualities she wanted in a second partner too. "They don't seem to like to change, outside of the big stores." She noticed Claire was staring at her. "What?"

"Not to be impolite but your slowing me down. If you want to chat, you could give me a hand."

"Oh yeah, sorry." Kelly liked this take charge side of Claire too. Kelly pushed the full cage to the doorway and brought an empty cage back for her.

"Thanks." Claire said as she wiped the sweat of her brow.

"I phoned Tony and organised tonight." Claire nodded. "I kind of got to thinking..." Kelly hesitated.

"What?" Claire asked.

"Do you think tonight, you can maybe, you know, be a bit more, well feminine." Kelly stood back a little.

Claire paused briefly. "I am, who I am. I thought you liked that." She was a little a bit concerned that Kelly was trying to put her in a box of conventional womanhood.

"Oh I do. I was mostly thinking of Tony. It might help grease the cogs a bit with him." Kelly explained.

"I don't understand." Claire said as she finished another cage. Their conversation was briefly interrupted as Kelly swapped them over.

"Well, he'll find you physically attractive himself for one thing and well I'm supposed to be showing him that I'm bisexual, that I enjoy sex with a woman. It's a bit difficult to show that with someone who looks like..." She bit her lip.

"With a someone who looks like what exactly?" Claire briefly stopped again. She expected Kelly to use some cringey overly macho cliche.

"Like a good looking member of a boy-band." Kelly said timidly as she twisted her hips nervously from side to side. Claire suspected she was trying to flatter her. In Claire's mind Kelly did a pretty decent job of making her feel good about herself.

"That's how you picture me?" Claire asked, finding it a little hard to believe. Kelly smiled and nodded. "Wow." Was all she could say. Claire knew Kelly found her attractive but had no idea how much. "Okay, I'll see what I can do. I'm not making any promises."

Kelly leaned over and kissed her on the lips. Claire could feel Kelly's hand brush against her crotch. "It's going to be great." Kelly told her.
1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.title: The shift continues

choices: Liz and Lizzy gets back home
The shift finishes up in the shop
Mike and the Natalies get ready for their night out
1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Unlike the hardware store, the salon had been quiet. Although Carly had only a few hours work to do, she wanted it over so she could get back to the flat. It had seemed like an eternity but at long last she was on her way home.

She got off her bus at her stop and as she walked to her flat, her mind started to wander. She wondered what kind of state the place would be in after she had requested that Liz and Lizzy should act amicably towards each other. She half expected to find that they had killed each other, either that or they would be in bed together having passionate sex.

Carly smiled as she realised that last thought was actually her having a sexual fantasy. She felt a little guilty about it, not about Lizzy just Liz. Why did one of her closest friends have to be almost identical to her lover? Also since discovering the truth about her sexuality, why was she having so many fantasies about women she knew? Probably because she could, she speculated.

She still didn't fully understand all the implications either. She knew stereotypically lesbian relationships normally consisted of a feminine woman who took on the 'traditional' submissive role and a more masculine partner who took on the dominant role. Even though they weren't in a relationship as such, she wondered if her and Lizzy fit that pattern. Looking down at herself she saw her very fashionable dress and her long varnished finger nails. She thought about how Liz typically dressed in a hoodie and jeans.

In her mind, it looked like it might be the case. Liz wasn't very butch though, she was more of a tomboy, she could still be quite feminine when she wanted. Again she realised she was fantasising about Liz not Lizzy. Maybe Liz hated that tomboyish part of herself and Lizzy would be less that way inclined.

Lizzy and her were going to have to have some long conversations, so she could get to really know her, especially now as she didn't have to pretend to be Liz in front of her.

She reached the security door of her building and hoped for the best.

---

When she opened the door of the flat, she immediately heard laughter. That's a good sign, she thought. She listened to the conversation as she removed her coat.

"So, I spoke to Luis in Spanish. I think he was impressed." One voice said. Carly easily worked out that was Lizzy.

"You big show off!" The other identical voice said.

Carly walked into the room. Both women were still sat in their previous positions. Assuming they hadn't swapped clothes to mess with her but she dismissed that idea as silly. "I hope you aren't going to tell Liz, everything about last night." Carly said as she sat down beside Lizzy. She gave her lover a peck on the cheek.

"Well, we do have to share information to keep our stories straight." Lizzy said with a huge grin. Carly pouted a little. "I suppose I could skip some of the more intimate details."

"Oh please do." Liz said, still laughing a little. "I don't really want to hear all that stuff."

Lizzy put her hand on Carly's stocking covered knee. "How was work?" She asked.

"Quiet. Zoe was a saint as usual. I don't know why she puts up with my antics." She looked at the two women. "Enough about me, how have you two been getting on?"

"Well," said Liz, "we have made some plans but they are somewhat dependent on you."

"Me?" Carly asked as she pointed at herself, feeling a little surprised.

"Yes, you." Lizzy said. "First off, we assumed that you'd be happy with me being your girlfriend."

"Really?" Carly asked.

"Yes, really." Lizzy said. The next thing she knew Carly's lips were on her own, she could feel the shorter woman's tongue in her mouth.

Liz groaned. "Don't get too excited. You don't know what you're signing up for yet." She warned.

Carly pulled away from Lizzy. "What do you mean?"

It took Lizzy a few seconds to regain her composure after the kiss attack. "I was hoping," Lizzy paused. Carly turned to face her again, "that I could move in with you."

Carly blinked for a few seconds. "Oh God, of course." Carly's lips were on hers again.

Liz shook her head. "Chuffing hell Carly. Do you think we can get through more than one sentence before the snogging session starts again?" She asked.

Carly pulled away again. "Sorry, I'm just so very happy." She leaned back against the sofa. "Wait a minute. What about Eric?" She asked Lizzy.

"We were coming to that." Lizzy said as she wiped Carly's lipstick off her mouth. "I'm sorry Carly, I can't change the way I feel. I still love him."

It was clear Carly was a little disappointed, although Lizzy's feelings didn't come as a big surprise to her.

"Thankfully," Liz said as she point to Lizzy, "she is a lot more open-minded when it comes to sex that me. Carly, if this is going to work you will have to be too."

"I don't understand." Carly said.

Lizzy held her hand. "How do you feel about sharing me?" Carly tilted her head. "Liz is going to be living with Eric most of the time as normal but she's agreed," Lizzy took a breath, "that I can take her place at home, one weekend every other week. I'll be Eric's wife and Ethan's mother for those two days." She was really wishing Carly wouldn't be jealous about it.

"I see." Carly said without emotion.

"There's more." Liz said. Carly turned to face her. "I'll need somewhere to live for those two days. I was hoping I could stay here." Carly's eyes darted towards the bedroom.

"You mean, with me?" Carly asked wondering if Liz wanted to share her bed too.

Liz picked up on the implication. "No, no. I just meant crashing on the sofa." She looked at it. "It folds out, right?"

"Yes." Carly said, again there was a hint of disappointment in her voice.

"This isn't set in stone either." Lizzy said. "Other things may crop up, school stuff, holidays, birthdays, things like that. We were just going to plan it as it crops up. You know, improvise." She explained.

Carly mulled it over in her head. "Birthdays?" She asked.

Lizzy nodded. "Well, we'll both probably stay at home, I mean Liz's house on Ethan's birthday."

"How's that going to work?" Carly asked, very confused. Liz and Lizzy looked at each other.

Liz spoke first. "We're going to tell Eric and Ethan the truth. I had planned on doing that anyway."

"They deserve it." Lizzy said. "If Liz and I are taking turns working, it would be fairly hard to explain why we were at home so much. It's just easier all round."

"So Eric will have to agree to this deal too?" Carly asked.

Liz smiled. "I think I, I mean we, can persuade him." She glanced at Lizzy before looking back at Carly. "So what do you say?"

The room was in silence for a few minutes before Carly responded as she thought their proposal over. "I did want you two to hammer out a solution." She paused again. "This is certainly, a solution. It isn't what I expected but I'm not sure what I was expecting." Liz and Lizzy both leaned over in anticipation. Carly smiled. "Sure, lets do this!"

It was Lizzy's turn to lean over and give Carly a long and protracted kiss. Liz watched them. As much as she made fun of them, she was happy that they were happy.

Liz started to fidget a little as the kiss continued. It was starting to feel uncomfortable, watching them for this long. She stood up. "I'll go put the kettle on, we'll have a quick cuppa before we head home." She said in attempt to break the current silence. Neither woman responded.

1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Eric and Ethan arrived home somewhat earlier than expected. They were sat in the kitchen talking to Courtney about their trip to see Reece.

Eric was talking away about meeting Reece's new girlfriend. Beside him at the kitchen table, Ethan was eating a rather large chocolate biscuit. He was largely being ignored by his father and his sister as they were engrossed in their conversation.

"So," said Eric concluding the story, "I didn't want to feel like we were intruding. That's why we're back early."

"I see." said Courtney as she took a sip of her water. It seemed Reece's new girlfriend was acting a little possessive while Eric was there.

Before any more could be said about the matter, they heard the front door open. Everyone turned their heads to see Lizzy walk into the kitchen. Her and Liz hadn't expected the early return of her husband and son. They had planned on briefing Courtney about the plan. After seeing the car outside they had to improvise.

Courtney stared in surprise. She knew she was looking at Lizzy as she was still wearing Liz's work clothes.

"Oh, hi Liz." Eric smiled as he noticed the work clothes too. "The shop didn't call you in today, did they?" Lizzy shook her head just as Ethan made a lunge to hug her. Lizzy expertly dodged it.

"You can hug me after you've finished that biscuit and washed all that chocolate off your hands." Lizzy told him in a stern tone. Ethan sighed and continued eating the biscuit at an increased rate. Lizzy walked round the kitchen a little.

"Someone was looking for you." Courtney told her as she made a face away from her dad, trying to secretly tell Lizzy about Liz.

"Oh, they found me." Lizzy said, just as the front door slammed shut.

"Who is that?" Eric asked assuming correctly, that whoever it was had travelled with Lizzy.

Courtney nearly spat out a mouthful of water, when Liz walked into the kitchen. She was carrying some shopping bags. "Hi everyone." There were now four adults and one child in the kitchen. "Isn't this cosy." She remarked about the lack of space.

Eric stared silently as he tried to comprehend what was going on.

Without warning, Ethan began crying. "What's wrong sunshine?" Lizzy asked. He was too upset to speak and merely pointed at the floor. The surprise of seeing a second mother had made him drop his biscuit on the floor. Lizzy grabbed another from the packet and he practically snatched it from her hands. The tears quickly receded as he began eating the new biscuit. She turned to look at the group. "We shouldn't be encouraging him, he won't eat his dinner now."

"Courtney?" Eric asked his daughter.

"Yes dad." She replied.

"I think there may be something wrong with me." He look worried. "I hope I'm not having a stroke."

"Can you spell burnt toast?" Courtney asked. Eric shook his head. "That's a good sign."

"Yes but I can see Liz in two different places." He pointed at Lizzy. "She's standing beside Ethan." He turned and pointed at Liz. "I can also see her at the kitchen door. I must be hallucinating."

"Err..., dad?" Courtney said.

"Yes?"

"You're not hallucinating. There are two Lizs in here, well kind of."

Eric sighed with relief. "Thank goodness for that. For a minute there I thought something strange was going on."

"Two mummies." Ethan agreed as he finished his biscuit.

Lizzy grabbed a damp cloth and wiped his little hands, then his face. "I think, the three of us," she pointed at Courtney and Ethan, "should go into the living room. I think Liz needs a long talk with Eric."

Lizzy gently grabbed Ethan's hand and pulled him towards the door. Courtney followed behind them as they walked past Liz.

Liz put her bags down and sat beside her husband. She put her hand on his.

Eric spoke quietly. "Must be a mid-life crisis. A few of the guys at work have had them."

Liz smiled. "It's not a mid-life crisis." She assured him. Depending on how he reacted it might turn into one, she thought.

"What's going on Liz?" He asked, looking confused.

"I've got quite the story for you." She said.

---

Lizzy sat on the sofa. Her arm was over Ethan's shoulder as he sat beside her. His head was leaning against her side.

Courtney was sitting restlessly in one of the chairs. "You could have warned me." She said.

"Sorry, we were planning on it. We didn't expect Eric to be back so soon." She explained.

"Planned?" Courtney asked, sounding a little surprised. Lizzy nodded. "You and Liz got together and actually planned something?" Lizzy nodded again. Courtney was impressed.

"Yes, well quite a lot has changed since yesterday." Before she could explain Ethan interrupted.

"Are you magic, mummy?" He asked as his child logic tried to get around the fact he had two mums. Lizzy and Courtney both smiled.

"You could say that Sunshine." Lizzy told him.

"It's not like Alison and Susan." He said referring to identical twins in his class at school.

"No, not like them." She thought it was quite clever of him to make the comparison. "You'll understand when you are older." She tried to explain.

"Okay." He said, reasonably satisfied with the explanation.

---

Nearly fifteen minutes later and Liz finished telling Eric about Lizzy. About the items from the future, the construct-o-matic, creating her, how Lizzy was different, falling out with her and making up again. She missed out some of the more salacious details. She didn't want to bring those up, until she was sure he was comfortable with the basics.

She had told him about the deal they'd reached with Carly.

Eric head was resting on his hand. "I don't know Liz. This is a lot to take in and on top of that she wants to spend two days a fortnight pretending to be you, including in our bed."

"She's going to be pretending to be me at work too. It's what she does." Liz explained.

"This is different!" Eric said angrily. "You want me to take a stranger into my bed and pretend she's my wife."

"It's not pretending to her, she has the same feelings for you, that I do." Liz paused. It wasn't the same thing, she knew that. "She's more understanding than me. She put me in touch with stuff I was oblivious to." She touched Eric's hand. "She's not as big a stranger as you think either."

"What do you mean?" Eric asked suspiciously.

"Do you think it was a coincidence Mistress L suddenly appeared the day Lizzy was created?" Liz asked, referring to Lizzy's dominatrix alter ego.

"You mean?" The realisation hit Eric hard. He looked horrified. "That was her?" Liz nodded. "You told her to do that with me?" Eric asked. He worried that it may have been some sort of twisted game on Liz's part.

"No, she did it herself because she loves you and knows how much you are into it." Liz explained. "I wasn't too happy about it, at first myself. I see things a bit differently now." She could see Eric still looked unhappy.

"I ended up stuck in those stupid cuffs." He said.

Liz couldn't help smiling. "Yeah, Courtney and Ethan coming home early threw a spanner in the works."

"So what happened to Lizzy when they got home?" Eric asked.

"Nothing, I got stuck in the cupboard under the stairs."

"What?"

"Once I discovered what was going on with you and Lizzy. I agreed to take her place as Mistress L. Just after I got into the outfit, the kids came home." Lizzy shoved me under the stairs. Eric laughed when he heard this. He actually got some pleasure from Liz's misfortune while he was stuck in the handcuffs.

"Sorry Liz, didn't mean to laugh."

"Yes, well you do realise you actually spent all Thursday night with Lizzy and never noticed she wasn't me?" Liz asked.

"Ah."

"Maybe I should be the offended one." She had him worried slightly but smiled eventually, indicating she wasn't serious. She could still see uncertainty on his face. "Look, I know this isn't easy for you to accept. Lizzy and I have something in mind that will help convince you."

"You do?" Eric asked.

"Yes, we just need to get rid of the kids."

1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Back at the store Steven discovered a new problem as he was going through his procedure for closing the business for the night. He couldn't reach the roller shutters that covered the windows. He made a few attempts but they were well out of his reach. He could hear laughter behind him. Distinctly male laughter. He quickly spun round with an angry look on his face.

The laughing co-workers all immediately shut up. Steven decided to look for one of the stools they used to stack the high shelves but they all appeared to have vanished. Steven suspected it was a deliberate attempt by certain members of staff to annoy him.

"Why don't you use this?" A female voice said. Steven spun round to see Kiran standing there. The young woman was holding a pole about three feet long with a hook on the end. Even at her modest height of five foot she was much taller than Steven.

"Thanks." He said as he took the pole from her. It crossed his mind, that using this hook was how he normally closed the shutters, in this version of reality.

"You don't have to try and prove anything you know." Kiran said as Steven hooked the first shutter.

"What?" He asked as he tried pulling downwards but nothing was happening.

"I've noticed you've changed outfits. I like the new clothes. I think they suit you better." Steven smiled. "I just thought you may have had a change of attitude."

He was a little confused. He didn't understand why Kiran was being as friendly as she was. "Sorry, it's just when you, me and Allie chat about this stuff, you seem so frustrated sometimes."

"I don't understand." It appeared he had some sort of new friendship with Allie and Kiran and he had no idea why.

"You can't help the way you were born. We have to try and deal with the world as it is. Fighting it, just wastes energy. I'm sure really tall people go through the same thing in reverse."

Steven understood now. He had a friendship with Allie and Kiran because of being short. It was something they had in common.

Steven pulled the pole with all his might and the shutter came down a lot faster than he was expecting. He fell backwards on to his small backside. "Oww!" He squealed. More laughter could be heard.

Kiran helped him get up. "Forget what I said, there are plenty assholes in here I'd love to fight." Steven got to his feet and brushed off the dust. Kiran leaned in close and spoke quietly. "Just to let you know. People have been saying stuff about you, again."

"Like what?" Steven asked as he moved to the next shutter.

Kiran looked around before speaking again. "They said you haven't got," she paused, "a dick." She turned bright red.

"It's true, I don't." He said as he reached up and hooked the shutter.

"Wow, after all these years, I would never have guessed." Steven found that difficult to believe. "So you're like a woman down there, then?" She pointed downwards.

Steven was amazed how many people this came as a total surprise to. "Yeah," he pointed at his breasts, "and up here too." He said sarcastically as he pulled the shutter down. He managed to judge the force better this time and was able to remain standing.

"Have you ever, you know, dressed as a woman?" Kiran asked. Steven thought these were very personal questions but they did have a closer relationship than he remembered.

"I don't think so." He honestly had no idea if he had.

"Your hair is a bit short but I think you could pull it off." She said earnestly.

Steven had briefly considered it earlier when he had looked at his reflection. He moved to the next shutter. "But I'm a guy." Was all he said. He could already feel his female psyche coming to life again.

"We should totally do it." Kiran said excitedly. "Next time we have a night out. We'd have to let Nat know as well." She said referring to Allie's partner. "She usually comes out with us. She works in the salon, she can help doll you up."

It was all a bit too much for him. "I'm not sure Kiran." He worried the universe might implode or something.

"It'll be a laugh. Don't worry, we'll go down to Sheffield or up to Doncaster, nobody would recognise you there."

"I'll think about it." Steven said as he hooked the next shutter.

1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Liz practically threw some money at Courtney as she asked her to take Ethan out for dinner. Ethan wasn't too keen on the idea until he found out he was going to his favourite clown themed burger place. Liz wasn't entirely happy about it herself considering he'd probably been eating junk all day with Eric.

After they left, she quickly disappeared upstairs to get ready for her little surprise.

Lizzy relaxed on the sofa. She felt comfortable here. As much as she enjoyed Carly's company, this place still felt like home. She knew Liz would be a while so she picked up the remote for the television. As she was about to turn it on, she heard someone clear their throat. She turned her head to see Eric awkwardly standing at the doorway to the living room.

They hadn't really spoken since she had arrived. "Err, can I get you something to drink?" He asked nervously as he tried to be a good host.

"No thanks, I had far too much last night. A coffee will be fine Eric." She said.

"What do you take in it?" He asked her.

She stared at him for a few seconds. "One sweetener and loads of milk."

Eric chuckled. "Liz takes the same." She glanced at him silently. He felt a little foolish. "Of course. She would do." He disappeared off towards the kitchen.

He returned a few minutes later with two coffees. He placed one on the coffee table, in front of her. He went to sit in the furthest chair from her.

At least he's in the same room, that's an improvement Lizzy thought. "I don't bite Eric, well not normally." She tapped her hand on the sofa next to her.

Hesitantly he got up and sat down beside her. Lizzy could feel the tension in him. "Relax, Eric." She said. That comforted him a little.

He leaned in towards her. "My God, you even smell like her." He said, seemingly at random.

"I have had these clothes on for two days. I did shower earlier."

"Sorry, I...," He trailed off.

"I don't know exactly how much Liz has told you but I've been acting like her, around you before. You have nothing to be nervous about."

"I feel like a fool. All that stuff we did, I should have known you weren't Liz and I didn't. I'm a terrible husband."

Lizzy was getting a strange feeling of deja-vu. She'd sat in almost exactly this same position a few nights ago, listening to Liz criticise her own marriage skills. She feared that without help their marriage was doomed to failure. "Well, it's a good thing I'm here. I'll make you a better husband and Liz a better wife."

"I am not sure how. You're basically Liz, right?" Eric asked.

"Basically but I'm also more. The longer I exist the better I understand it. Especially after that mass of stupid directives that clouded my thoughts was removed." Eric didn't fully understand but he continued to listen. "My enhancements were designed to allow me to do the things Liz was unable or unwilling to do. That's all bullshit, my enhancements are to help make those around me happier and have more fulfilling lives." There had been hints bleeding through her thoughts but she finally understood the truth about herself. Her life had purpose.

"I couldn't have put it better myself." A voice said from behind Eric. Lizzy smiled as Eric spun round.

Mistress L stood in the doorway. Dressed exactly as she was the other night. The red heels, the fishnet tights, the red PVC swimsuit and the elbow length gloves. Her hair flowed around her butterfly mask. She had tried to sneak down the stairs as quietly as possible. Eric stared at her large partially exposed breasts.

Lizzy grinned. "You look fantastic Mistress L." She told her.

Liz smiled. "Thank you dear." She turned to Eric. "Why are you wearing clothes slave?" She asked him.

Eric stared at her. "I didn't know, I was..."

"Unsatisfactory answer!" Liz told him harshly. "I'll be upstairs in five minutes. If you are naked on the bed by then, I might reconsider..., the punishment."

Eric practically leapt out the sofa and ran out the room. They heard him thunder up the stairs.

Liz looked at Lizzy and lifted the mask. "He's like a big kid. It's good to see him enjoy life."

"Are you up for this?" Lizzy asked her, a little concerned. "I could take over, if you're not."

"Oh no, this is my turn. You'll get yours." She told her sharply. Lizzy smiled, glad Liz was feeling more confident. Liz bit her lip. "I would be lying if I said I wasn't nervous though."

"You'll be great." Lizzy said, reassuring her.

Liz replaced her mask and left the room.

He slipped out of bed and quietly got dressed.

"No, I don't think that's really an option. I'll get them to sit down and I'll calmly explain the situation." She smiled. "At least they know I don't have a sister."

"Okay, don't rub it in." Mike was still feeling embarrassed about assuming she had a twin.

Before the conversation could progress any further, Natalie's phone buzzed. She picked it up and looked at the screen.

"Who is that?" Mike asked as she read it.

"Roni. You know that woman from Leeds," Natalie explained.

"Yeah, the one from the hen party." Mike was surprised she wasn't hungover too. She'd been pretty drunk when she had spoken to them and that had been quite early on.

Claire woke up to the sounds of someone quietly humming a tune. She opened her eyes and saw an unfamiliar flesh coloured blob moving around at the bottom of the bed. Looking to her side she noticed the bed was empty. Sitting up, she grabbed her glasses from the bedside cabinet where she vaguely remembered putting them the previous night.

Sitting up, Claire focused her eyes. She saw the blob was actually Molly dancing in front of the large mirror attached to the dressing table. She was wearing just a pair of shorts and Claire's discarded wig on her head. Claire found the whole scene a little surreal and suspected she might still be dreaming.

Claire became aware of her own nakedness and she looked downwards. Although the covers had fell down the were still above her waist. She breathed a sigh of relief that her secret was still hidden.

Molly spotted that Claire was awake in the mirror's reflection and spun round. "Morning," she said. "You look totally different now."

Claire remembered Kelly had said something similar yesterday. Like mother, like daughter, Claire thought. She decided to play along. "I'm a master of disguise, the woman of a thousand faces," she said. It was a cliché but it actually hit home with Claire. She often felt she was looking for an identity of her own, a place in the world.

Molly response was to giggle. "You're silly," she told her.

"There you are!" Kelly said as she walked into the room. She was wearing some leggings and a pink tee shirt. She had some clothes in her hand and looked a little out of breath. She turned to Claire. "This place is so big it can take me hours to track her down. Your door couldn't have been shut properly, which someone," she pointed at Molly, "took as an open invitation to invade your privacy."

Claire chuckled. "It's okay, she wasn't bothering me," she said.

Kelly pulled the wig off Molly's head and put it on the dressing table. She again pointed a finger at Molly. "I'm guessing you didn't ask permission to take that," Kelly said to her.

"I couldn't ask for permission, Auntie Claire was asleep," Molly said, using childish logic to justify her actions.

The fact she was referring to her as an aunt didn't go unnoticed by Claire. Kelly spotted the surprise in Claire's face before turning back to Molly. "You are supposed to ask Claire if it is okay to call her that first."

Molly gave her mother a confused look. "But you said–" she said before Kelly interrupted her.

"Never mind that now."

It was clear to Claire that Kelly and Molly had been discussing her privately again. Kelly attempted to distract Molly by pulling a white tee shirt over her head. She grabbed one of Molly's arms in attempted to get it in a sleeve.

Molly shook her off. "Muuum!" she whined. "I can dress myself." She put her own arm in the sleeve.

Kelly shook her head. "You wouldn't know it, the way you saunter around here like Lady Godiva," she told her.

Molly finished putting on the tee shirt. "Who is she?" she asked.

"She's a famous woman who rode a horse through Coventry, naked," Kelly explained.

Molly looked deep in thought. "Aren't horses usually naked?" she asked her mother innocently.

Both Claire and Kelly couldn't stop themselves from smiling. Kelly put on a serious face. "I meant Lady Godiva was naked."

"Oh," Molly said realising her mistake, "she sounds cool."

"Yes, now go get your breakfast and leave Claire in peace."

Molly slumped her shoulders and walked slowly out the room. Kelly looked out the door to make sure she went downstairs before turning back to Claire. "Sorry about that. She's curious about you," Kelly said.

"She didn't seem too curious yesterday."

"She was a bit shy then, now she's got used to you," Kelly said ominously.

Claire decided to change the subject. "I don't even remember falling asleep," she said.

Kelly lowered her voice. "Tony went through to bed, then I went to clean up after the sex and by the time I got back you were out like a light," she said.

"Wow! Sorry." Claire felt terrible.

"I don't blame you. You were working like an ox in the warehouse yesterday, with that and the sex you must have been exhausted."

"I must have been." Thinking back it had seemed like a long day to Claire even though they had went to bed relatively early.

"I was up for some more baby making too," Kelly said, teasing Claire a little.

"Sorry." Claire felt bad again.

"It's okay. Tony kept me satisfied. He was needing some attention after our little show. I just ended up going to sleep in my own bed." She moved forward towards the bed. "That's okay isn't it?" she asked, sounding a little concerned.

"Sure, I was sleeping anyway. Is Tony downstairs?" Claire asked.

Kelly shook her head. "Sunday league football."

Claire was surprised, she didn't get the impression he was particularly physical person. "He plays?"

Kelly shook her head. "Goodness no, he's a linesman. It's just an excuse for a piss up."

"I see."

"I've made some breakfast, if you want it," Kelly said.

"Sounds good," Claire said feeling a little hungry, as she looked around for the pieces of her outfit.

"You don't have to get dressed, I brought this," Kelly told her as she dropped a red dressing gown on the bed. "It's a bit short on me though."

Claire spun round and put her feet on the floor. She stood up and immediately felt Kelly's eyes on her naked body. She pulled on the dressing gown which failed to conceal her penis. "Yeah, pretty short," Claire agreed.

"Personally, I'm not complaining but you better put these on," Kelly said as she handed her a pair of blue shorts. "I hope you don't mind. They are a pair of Tony's trunks."

"Sexy," Claire said, slightly sarcastically as she pulled them on.

—

The three of them sat around the kitchen table eating breakfast. Kelly had laid out a wide selection of dishes. Claire normally had a far more modest breakfast, if she even bothered at all. It all felt very domestic to Claire and she sort of liked it. They didn't talk much and they enjoyed the food quietly until Kelly had something to say.

"Tony and I got to talking last night, " she said to Claire.

"Oh?" Claire managed to say, as she ate a mouthful of croissant. She wondered where Kelly was going with this.

"It's just after what you said about disliking your flat. We wondered if maybe, you'd consider..." Kelly trailed off, seemingly nervous about finishing the question.

"What?" A now curious Claire asked.

"Moving in here with us?"

This was all very sudden for Claire. It was clear her and Kelly had chemistry but their current relationship was less than a day old. It was a great opportunity though, to be a part of a loving and caring family. It was something she hadn't known she needed.

She sat there silently, not sure what to say. She did notice Molly was staring in anticipation as well. Claire assumed she must have known that Kelly was going to ask her.

"Err, well," Claire told them. It was all she could manage to say as she tried to process her emotions.

Kelly misread it as reluctance. She spoke eagerly, "You said you liked the place, we wouldn't charge you rent or anything, you could save that. If you just contributed a bit to the bills that would be cool. If we are on shift together I could give you a lift into work..." Kelly continued to rattle off a million reasons why it was a good idea.

In Claire's mind Kelly's words quickly became a meaningless mumble. She felt her heartbeat speed up, starting to feel like a cornered animal. As Kelly continued to drone on, the feelings got worse. It was like claustrophobia. "Stop!" she practically shouted at Kelly.

Kelly stopped speaking and Claire started to relax again. Kelly looked a little surprised briefly before it turned into a look of concern. "I'm pushing you again, aren't I?" she asked.

With great restraint, Claire answered, "A little bit yes." She felt her peaceful equilibrium slowly return. "It's not just about me and you is it?"

"What do you mean?" Kelly asked.

"Well, there's Molly to consider," Claire said. "How does she feel about me moving in. She hardly knows me and she doesn't even know what I'm doing here." She took a sip of her drink.

Molly looked at Claire and matter-of-factly said, "You are going to help mum and dad make me a new brother or sister."

Claire nearly spat out her orange juice. She was taken aback by just how in depth Kelly had explained the situation to Molly.

"See," Kelly told Claire, "she knows about it. It wasn't like we were going to be able to keep it a secret from little miss nosy for long anyway."

Molly continued to look at Claire. "So how do you–" she started to ask before Kelly interrupted her again.

"You don't need to know that!" Kelly practically shouted. Molly looked frightened. Kelly calmed down a little when she realised how aggressive she'd been. "You'll find out when you are older. Just be happy that with Claire's help the stork will hopefully be dropping a baby down the chimney soon."

Molly gave a condescending laugh. "I'm not stupid mum, that's not where babies come from," she said.

Claire was again surprised. "You know where babies come from?" she asked Molly before she realised what she had said.

"Yeah," Molly rolled her eyes like it was the most obvious thing in the world, "they come from the hospital." The naive girl proudly told them both.

Claire, Kelly and Molly had not long finished breakfast when they heard the distant front door slam shut. Kelly tilted her head looking a little surprised as Anthony soon walked into the kitchen.

"You're back early," Kelly said.

Anthony nodded looking a little unhappy. He kissed the top of Molly's head and then gave Kelly a peck on the cheek before sitting down. He gave a long sigh. "One team didn't have enough players, again," he said, sounding a little depressed.

"So what happened?" Kelly asked.

"After the team without enough players forfeited, the lads just had a quick five-a-side game and went home."

"Shame," Kelly said. Her expression changed to a smile. "So you won't be going to the pub then?" she asked.

"I never said that. I just wasn't going to sit around a cold playing field waiting for opening time."

"Uh-huh. So you'll be disappearing again in a couple of hours."

Anthony felt a little sheepish. "Yeah, just for a few," he admitted.

Kelly turned to Claire. "And the rest," she said, teasing him a little. Kelly saw an opportunity arise and turned to Molly. "Hey Molly do you want to do some colouring in?" she asked her daughter.

Molly shook her head. "Nah," she said nonchalantly, which scuppered Kelly's plan.

"You can do it in the living room," Kelly told her.

Molly lifted her head and seemed more interested. She hadn't been allowed to do that activity in there for some time. She was clever enough to know when her mum wanted to do something for her own reasons. "I'm not sure," she said. This was turning into a negotiation.

"I'll put on a new film on the Sky box for you." Kelly knew that anything involving spending money on Molly was guaranteed to change her mind.

Molly's face lit up. "Yeah!" she said. She grabbed her colouring pens and book, that were still in the dining room before running down the hall towards the living room.

"I'll be back." Kelly told the others as she followed Molly down the hall.

Anthony turned to Claire. "That girl has Kelly twisted around her little finger. She won't know what hit her when she's not an only child any more," he said.

"Yeah," Claire agreed. She knew a new baby would disrupt Molly's life. She hoped for the better.

"So has Kelly discussed our little proposal?" he asked, looking at her somewhat expectantly.

"She has, I think she might have got the wrong end of the stick though."

"Oh?"

"I want to consider it for a little while. Kelly thinks I am against the idea. She started going on about stuff." She paused. "I told her to back off." Anthony looked surprised. "In as nice a way as possible," she clarified. "It's all moving too fast for me and I want to do this properly, not half-arsed."

"Of course," he said, "She's frightened she's going to lose you." He lowered his voice. "I always knew it was a possibility that some day you'd want to move in. Which is fine, by the way, we've got more spare room here than we'll ever need. I was happy to leave the when up to you. I just told Kelly it was okay to ask you because I wanted to help reassure her." Claire nodded. "Do you want me to talk to her about it?" he asked.

"No, no. I think she kind of got the message," she said, just before Kelly walked back into the room.

Kelly looked at them both. "You talking about me, again?" she asked. Both Anthony and Claire looked a little guilty.

Anthony felt brave enough to talk about it. "It's only fair, we've spoken privately about Claire," he said.

"Not that much," Kelly said.

"You've coached Molly so much about Claire, I'm surprised you didn't give her a rule book instead," he said.

It was Kelly's turn to look guilty. "Yes, well," she said awkwardly, "speaking of Molly you better get in there and watch her before she destroys the wallpaper again."

"She's older now and I think she learnt her lesson," Anthony assured her.

"I'm not as sure about that as you, Tony," she said as she pointed towards the hall. "So get in there and keep an eye on her."

Anthony stood up. He looked between both of them. "I get the picture," he said. "You two are like blinkin' rabbits." He turned and headed off towards the living room.

Natalie's parents Tim and Sandra sat on the small sofa in Natalie and Mike's living room. Both were in their late fifties and had lived in the area their entire lives.

Mike stood awkwardly in front of the couple while Harry was at his feet playing with one of his toy cars. The only one speaking was Harry.

"Brrrm! Brrrm!" he said as he pushed the car along the carpet.

They were currently waiting on Natalie. Mike knew the hold up was her hungover body which he suspected they were struggling to get dressed.

Tim looked at his watch before looking at Mike. "Is this important?" he asked.

"Very." Mike replied.

"Will it take long?"

"I don't think so."

"It's just football starts in an hour and–" Was all he got to say before Sandra nudged him, silencing him. Tim sighed and placed his hands in front of his slightly overweight belly.

The room fell into silence again, interrupted by thumping sounds from upstairs. Everyone looked upwards before returning their heads to their previous positions.

"Is it a surprise?" Sandra asked Mike excitedly.

"Oh, it will definitely be a surprise," Mike assured her.

Sandra smiled as she stroked her dyed blonde hair. "I like surprises," she told them.

"I like football," Tim said a little frustrated. "Did you see the match last night?" He asked Mike.

Mike shook his head. "No, Natalie and I went out in Doncaster," he informed them.

"Oh, Doncaster, fancy," Sandra said.

"Did you get a taxi?" Tim asked. Mike nodded. "I bet that cost you a bloody fortune. Rip off merchants," Tim grumbled. Tim was also a taxi driver, he often moaned about how he never got the longer more lucrative runs and was stuck doing trips in the local area. Mike knew better than to say anything, igniting that particular conversation, that he'd had many, many times with his father-in-law.

There was another thump on the ceiling. "Fuck sake!" Natalie could be heard shouting. It resonated clearly through the whole house.

Sandra started to get up. "Maybe she needs a hand," she said as she went to go help her daughter.

Mike in a near panic extended his arm in front of her. "No, she's fine," he said.

"Oh," Sandra said as she sat back down again.

Mike was greatly relieved when he eventually heard footsteps coming down the stairs. Natalie walked into the living room with a somewhat fake looking smile on her face. She was dressed in her regular leggings and tee-shirt.

"All sorted?" Mike asked. He could hear a second much quieter set of footsteps following down the stairs.

"Yeah," she said to him. She turned to her mum and dad. "Mum, dad I want you to understand the world around us is not as straightforward as it seems, strange things happen. Something happened to me the other day when–"

"Get to the point luv," her dad said interrupting her.

Natalie looked towards the door. Natalie's other body walked in slowly. Mike thought she looked like death warmed up. Her hair was a lot messier than the first Natalie. She stood beside her other body. Harry was totally oblivious, still playing with his car. "There's two of me now." She grinned awkwardly with both her faces.

Both her parents looked stunned. They turned to look at each other and back at the Natalies. "I have two bodies but I just have one mind," one of the Natalies said.

"How?" her father asked.

"These," Natalie held up both wrists that held the copper coloured bracelets. The green gems shimmered in the light. "I put it on and it splits me into two."

Her mother looked surprised. Her father started smiling. "I don't believe it," he said.

"It's true, honestly," a Natalie said, hoping she could convince him it was real. Mike nodded furiously in an attempt to confirm it.

"I believe that bit," he said, "It's pretty obvious." He turned to his wife, "Show them luv."

Sandra pulled up the long sleeve of her blouse and held up her slim wrist, in between several bangles was a bracelet similar to Natalie's. It was slightly more silver in colour and the gem was red.

It was Natalie and Mikes turn to looked stunned. "You mean?" a Natalie asked, eventually.

Sandra nodded. "I've had two bodies for the last twenty years," she confessed.

"Ha!" Mike shouted, he calmed down a little as he looked between the two confused Natalies, "I said it was a family heirloom."

1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
Everyone was looking at Sandra.

"But–" one Natalie said.

"–how?" the other finished.

"What luv?" A confused Sandra asked as she looked between the two of them.

The Natalie's faces showed some concentration. Only one spoke. "How did you get a bracelet? Where's your other body? How come you never told us–" Before Natalie could ask any more questions Sandra held up her palm, silencing her daughter.

"Why don't you sit down and I'll explain everything," Sandra said.

Mike sat on one armchair. One of Natalie's bodies sat on the other. The remaining body sat on the arm of Mike's chair and she put her arm around his shoulder. Mike could smell the faint scent of alcohol, so guessed it was the hungover body with him.

As people were moving around distracting him, Harry stopped playing with his car and paid attention. His confused eyes quickly moved between his mother's bodies several times. Unable to process what he was seeing, he went back to playing with his toy car. "Brrrm! Brrrm!" he said quietly.

Sandra tried to think back to the start of the story. She hadn't actually told it before. "Well, I guess it started in town. It was 1999," she said. Three sets of eyes were on her. "I was near where your work is," she said to one of Natalies, "and I met this really interesting couple."

"Did you recognise them?" the Natalie beside Mike asked.

Sandra shook her head. "No, never saw them before. They were dressed really odd. The man had on these strange glasses and the woman had a face covered in glitter." She pointed under her eyes. "They were a strange sight, I can tell you," she said as she opened her eyes wide. "It was early in the morning and I'd just come off the night shift. I was at the bus stop by myself and they come up to me while I was stood there. They said hello and that, then the bloke says he's from the year 2030 and he had a magical gift from the future for me." Sandra paused to let the others react to her revelation.

"So I thought," she said, "they been, you know." She mimed a drinking motion. "He sticks his hand in this grotty looking carrier bag and pulls out the bracelet." She held up her wrist again.

"It had two gems on it?" the Natalie sitting on their own asked.

Sandra nodded again. "Like rubies. So he hands it to me and says I better wait till I get home to put it on. I thought he was going to ask for money for it."

"Did he?" Mike asked.

"No, that was the strangest part. He pulled out a TV remote and the two of them just vanished. Disappeared right in front of me. So I drops the bracelet in my bag and I forgot all about it. I thought I'd been hallucinating through lack of sleep or something."

"I bet," the Natalie on her own agreed.

"So when I get home, I put the kettle on to make a cuppa–"

Tim interrupted her. "I could murder one right now," he said out loud.

Sandra gave him the look of death. "They are listening to my story," she told him.

"I'm missing the match for your life story," he complained, "and they don't both have to be here, do they?" He pointed at the two Natalies. "You know that."

Sandra still looked unhappy at her husband.

"It's all right mum," the Natalie by herself said, "dad is right." She got up and walked through to the kitchen.

"Can I continue?" a somewhat annoyed Sandra asked Tim.

"Don't let me stop you," Tim replied.

"So I was sitting there drinking my tea and I decided to have a look at the bracelet again. Seeing it in the proper light it was actually quite pretty looking. I saw it had a little sticker on it with two people drawn on it. I didn't know what it meant." She paused thoughtfully. "I do now of course."

"The sticker was rubbed off on mine," Natalie commented.

"So of course I tried it on," Sandra said, "and that's when the fun and games started. Next thing I know, I was on the kitchen floor and my leg was hurting. I think I must have dropped the hot tea on myself and burnt my leg. I screamed a bit."

"And woke me up," Tim added.

"And I woke your dad up," she said as she gave Tim another glare. "So there I was rubbing my thigh and I noticed there was this woman sitting in my chair."

"Hang on a minute mum," Natalie said as she leaned forward. Sandra stopped. "Weren't we in the house too?" Natalie asked. Sandra nodded.

"Typical teenagers," Tim said, "A bomb could have gone off and you'd have slept right through it." Natalie silently admitted to herself that her father was right.

Sandra nodded in agreement with her husband too. "So your father came down the stairs."

Mike spoke up. "And got the shock of his life too, I bet," he said, talking from personal experience.

"I was a tad surprised," Tim agreed. "It's not something you expect to see at the kitchen table at six o'clock in the morning."

Natalie carrying a tea tray walked back in. "What don't you expect to see at the kitchen table at six o'clock in the morning?" she asked, pretending not to have heard the rest.

"Ha, bloody ha," Tim said sarcastically.

The storytelling was interrupted briefly as Natalie distributed the tea to everyone. She returned to her seat.

Sandra took a sip of her tea. "So, your father checked I was all right," she said, "while mumbling something about twin sisters. He helps me get up and the strange woman was still sitting at the table, not moving."

"Strange woman?" Mike asked.

"She didn't recognise her own face," Tim said.

Sandra shrugged. "Well you don't expect to see your own face on someone else's body. Anyway I don't really understand what happened but the next thing I knew I was sitting at the table then I was back standing beside Tim. I kept swapping and it got faster and faster then," she pressed both her hands together quickly, "blam! I was in both places at the same time." She stopped talking.

"And then?" asked both Natalies at the same time.

"And then," Sandra said, "I took the bloody thing off, it was freaking me out. I didn't put it on again for days."

"You did put it on it again?" the Natalie next to Mike asked.

Sandra nodded. "Oh, yes I tried it a few times," she said. She looked at Tim and touched his hand. "Tried a few different things."

The Natalie beside Mike, rubbed his shoulder and smiled at him. Nothing was said but it was clear Tim and Sandra had done similar stuff to Natalie and Mike.

"But it made me ill," Sandra told them. "One time I wore it for nearly a day and when I took it off I thought it I was so sick, I thought I was going to die."

Natalie in the armchair confirmed it. "Liz at work thought it was confusion from everything I'd experienced getting putting back into one brain again."

"Yes, well, I stopped using it," she said. She took another sip of her tea. "Then they closed the factory."

"Huh?" Mike asked.

"The factory where I worked," Sandra explained. "They transferred production down South to London. I got offered a supervisor's position if I moved."

"I remember that!" the Natalie in the armchair said.

"I thought about it," Sandra admitted. "Don't get me wrong. I loved living in Rotherham, I've been here my whole life. Let's face it though, compared to London it's not that exciting. I remembered the bracelet and I thought, why not have my cake and eat it, as it were." She grinned. She looked at Tim again. "After I discussed it with Tim of course."

It was Tim's turn to touch Sandra's hand. "I doubt, I could have changed your mind anyway," he told her.

"Wait," Armchair Natalie said. "Are you saying you moved one of your bodies to London to go live down there and work in a factory?" she asked, totally amazed.

"Yes, luv," Sandra confirmed, "it's been quite the adventure. London is such fun."

"And I was worried about going to Leeds," the Natalie next to Mike said to him.

"What?" Sandra asked.

"Nothing mum, it's not important."

Mike finally catching up with the conversation said, "So your body has been down in London for twenty years?" He asked Sandra.

Sandra shook her head. "No, not quite, I took turns." she said.

"How?" Armchair Natalie asked.

"Skegness." Tim said.

"Your dirty weekends?" she asked her parents. Five or Six times a year her parents had a weekend away at the resort town, which lay roughly the same distance from Rotherham and London. Her parents made no secret of what they did there.

"We enjoy them," Tim said. "Nice big beds in them hotels." He smiled cheekily. This earned him a slap on the arm from Sandra.

"I'm sure they don't need to know about what we get up to," she admonished him.

"I'm sure Mike already does."

Mike grinned at his father-in-law.

Natalie searched her memory. The one in the armchair turned to her mother and said, "I often wondered why you always seemed to getting you hair done in Skegness."

"That was when I swapped," Sandra confirmed. "I wanted to tell you, especially as you got older but I was afraid you wouldn't believe me."

"I knew the bracelet looked familiar when I saw it. Now I know why," the Natalie beside Mike told him. "Now we know, this certainly opens up some interesting possibilities."

A look of concern grew on Mike's face as worried about what Natalie was thinking.

Lizzy was on the shop floor checking stock errors. She was humming an upbeat tune to herself, still in a good mood after her lunch with Carly when she heard a voice. "Liz!" it called, trying to attract her attention.

Lizzy turned around to see a young man smiling with a face covered in a short beard. He was somewhat short of stature and had a thinning hairline that resembled his father's.

Lizzy smiled back at him. "Reece! I wasn't expecting to see you today," she said with genuine surprise at meeting Liz's stepson. Lizzy quickly double-checked her memory to confirm this was the first time she'd actually met him.

"We needed a few bits and pieces for the flat, so we thought we'd pop in and get them," he said. Reece smiled broadly again, "How are you?" he asked in an enthusiastic manner.

"Good thanks," Lizzy informed him. She was a little puzzled though. "Not that I'm complaining but it's a long way to come for a 'few bits and pieces'," she said. She knew Reece and his girlfriend lived about thirty miles away.

Reece looked a little sheepish. "Well, you know, family staff discount," he said.

"Ah," said Lizzy before her cynicism caused her lips to press together. She thought he was being a bit friendlier than usual. Lizzy knew, they had never had the closest of relationships. She corrected herself mentally, yet again. Liz hadn't had a close relationship with her husband's son, she told herself.

Lizzy knew she'd built bridges with Courtney, so she felt she could do the same with Reece. "Well, that's what it's there for," she said. Normally Liz would be reluctant to let others take advantage of her company discount.

Reece looked at her expectantly. A smile, this time a more awkward one crossed his lips.

"Sure, go for it, just give me a shout when you get to the till," Lizzy told him.

"Thanks, Liz," he said before turning around and disappearing into the aisles.

—

Thirty minutes later and the shopping was paid for. Liz noted it was more than 'a few bits and pieces'. There was an extremely full trolley next to them.

She got to meet Reece's girlfriend Ruth. She was quite attractive and Lizzy thought that she seemed nice enough. Lizzy thought she had a very shapely pair of legs, which she'd had found herself glancing at more than she should have.

The three of them were standing near the tills.

"Thanks again Liz, " Ruth said to her. "It all helps when we're on a tight budget."

"No problem, luv. I know what it's like," she said. She wondered how sensible they actually were when it came to money. The extra fuel for the car journey would probably wipe out most of the discount, even on such a large amount of shopping. "So, you got anything else planned for today?" she asked, making conversation.

"We were going to pop over to see Eric," Ruth said casually.

"What?" Lizzy asked, a mild panic in her voice. "But, but you saw him yesterday."

Ruth frowned. "Yeah, I thought we'd maybe left a bad impression on him. I'd just came off a night shift and really wanted to sleep. We weren't being very good company. I felt really bad about it later," she explained.

"I see," Lizzy said, a little distracted. She was mentally processing her next step.

"And I haven't seen Court in ages. We can catch up." Reece added.

"I'm sure she'll be happy to see you. I'm sure Ethan will be too." A plan quickly formed in her mind. "Actually you know what, I might finish up early today and join you," she lied.

"You can do that?" Ruth asked, a little surprised.

"I am a manager. I can do these things. It's my prerogative," she lied again. She spotted Rob on the nearest till, give her a strange look when she said that. He couldn't quite believe his ears. Lizzy spotted him and skilfully moved between him and the couple.

"The more the merrier, I guess," Reece said, a little confused why his stepmother was so keen to skip work to come join them.

"But there's no rush, take your time. Explore the town centre, maybe visit the park." she told them in a desperate bid to buy time.

Reece had grown up in Rotherham, he had no idea why she wanted him to visit places he was familiar with.

Lizzy waved them off as they left. "Are you finishing early?" Rob asked her, wanting to confirm what he heard.

"In your dreams and stop being so chuffing nosey," a stressed Lizzy told him. Sinking his head down, Rob spun his till chair back towards the till.

She knew she needed to call Liz, to warn her about her stepson's imminent arrival . She stuck her hand in her pocket to grab her phone. Before she could retrieve it, there was an almighty crash from the back of the store.

"What now?" Lizzy wondered out loud as she walked quickly towards the source of the noise. The urgent phone call was forgotten about as the unexpected event grabbed her attention.
