1342421111111111111111111111111111111211.content:
"Hellooo!" Reece called out as he and Ruth walked in the front door on Liz and Eric's house. There was no verbal response but he was sure he heard footsteps rapidly moving around upstairs.

Ruth leaned over towards him. "Are you sure they'll be okay with us visiting?" she asked him quietly, "They aren't expecting us." she added.

He turned back towards her. "It's fine and I'm sure Liz has already called to tell them," he said with a slight touch of negativity in his tone. He'd always considered Liz a bit of a gossip. He had no idea what big secrets she had been keeping recently.

Ruth picked up on what Reece was implying and thought he was being a bit harsh on his stepmother. She had found her pleasant enough and she had got them a discount on their shopping.

They walked into the living room to find Courtney slouched in a chair, on her phone. Ethan was on the other side of the room. He was distracted for the time being, playing some children's games on his tablet.

"Hey Bro," Courtney said as she glanced up from her phone.

"Hi Court," he said with about as little emotion as she had used. He turned to his girlfriend, "This is my sister Courtney." He pointed at her before turning to back to Courtney while pointing at Ruth. "This is my girlfriend–"

"Roof!" Ethan squealed, noticing her presence. He put his tablet on the sofa before jumping off it, to greet her.

"Hi Ethan," she said as the boy hugged her legs, his standard greeting.

"Nice to meet you," Courtney said to Ruth as she tried to wrestle away the little boy.

"So I suppose Liz phoned you?" Reece asked his sister.

"Why would she phone?" she asked, fully aware Liz was upstairs. Between the noise of the Ethan squealing and her not understanding the question, she found it difficult to concentrate on what Reece was actually saying.

"We popped into her shop and spoke to her on the way here," he explained.

Courtney's eyes opened wide. "Oh, shit," she said quietly to herself.

"What?" Reece asked.

"Nothing," the panicking Courtney told them.

"What's all this racket?" Eric said as he walked in, looking at Ethan squealing.

"Roof! Is here." a delighted Ethan said before he started squealing again.

"Yes, I can see that son," he told him, "I can never get peace to get a decent lie-in around here."

"Roof! Roof! Roof!" Ethan screamed, inexplicably delighted by her presence. Right now, for him, nobody else in the room existed.

Reece cocked his head. "Dad, it's well into the afternoon," he said, as he tried to ignore his younger brother.

"Oh, really?" Eric asked innocently.

"I wanted to apologise about yesterday Eric," Ruth uttered her prepared apology. It was difficult as Ethan was still being quite noisy, "I had just finished my night shift," Eric nodded as he tried to listen, "and I was tired and..." She trailed off. Ethan shouting was distracting her too much.

"I understand, luv," Eric told her as he raised his voice to be heard while constantly glancing at the now hyperactive Ethan.

"Roof! Roof! Roof!" It didn't seem like Ethan would ever stop chanting her name.

"Shhhhh!!!!" Liz hissed loudly as she walked into the room to join them. Everyone fell silent, including Eric.

Both her and Eric had dressed very quickly when they heard Reece's voice a few minutes earlier. She was still pulling on her hoodie. "That's better," she announced, "We can hear ourselves think now." She looked around the living room.

She expected silence from Ethan and the submissive Eric but she hadn't expected it from Reece and a young woman who she presumed was Reece's girlfriend. Liz tried to recall her name. She was sure Eric mentioned it yesterday but in all the excitement, with Lizzy being there, it had slipped her mind. They both looked shocked to see Liz. Liz found herself staring at the woman's shapely legs for some reason. She shook the thought away.

Reece's ability to talk returned, "That was fast!" he told Liz.

"Fast?" the slightly confused Liz asked him. She thought he was maybe implying that he knew her and Eric had just been having sex.

"I knew you said your were finishing early but we weren't expecting you for a while yet," Reece told her, unable to fathom how Liz had got back from the shop so quickly. Liz had even managed to find time to change her clothes.

Liz took a step back, unsure of what he was talking about. She opened her mouth to talk before Courtney interrupted.

"Liz can sure move fast when she wants to," she said. Courtney was slowly putting it together. Obviously she couldn't say anything in front of her older brother and his girlfriend. "She knows all the short-cuts." Courtney's younger ears could now hear Liz's phone vibrating upstairs. She had a fairly good idea who was calling Liz.

"Is that my phone?" Liz asked, hearing it herself. Just then it stopped, "must be imagining things," she said, before she turned her attention back to Courtney. Just as Liz was about to speak, Courtney's phone began ringing.

Courtney looked at it, a little confused for a second before cancelling the call. "Unknown number," she said before turning to Liz. "Ended in one, one, six." Courtney didn't know for certain but suspected it was Lizzy.

Liz felt the number was familiar but couldn't place it. She shook her head.

"Maybe you should check your phone," Courtney suggested to Liz while making all sorts of strange faces, to hint that Lizzy was probably trying to contact her. Liz looked at her thoroughly befuddled. "Jeez," Courtney said feeling frustrated at Liz's obliviousness, "I'll go get it." She dived out of her chair before thundering out the room.

Liz shook her head, turning to Eric, she said,"I don't know what goes through that girl's brain sometimes." She turned back to the still somewhat surprised Reece and Ruth, "Now where were we?"

"How you got back here so quick from your work," Reece said.

"From my work?" Liz face regained a look of bewilderment. "What are you talking–" Was all she got to ask her stepson before Courtney shoved the phone in her face.

"There's a message for you," she gasped. She was breathless from her very quick trip up and down the stairs. "I think you should read it," she said with emphasis.

Liz found herself annoyed with Courtney's rudeness as she took the phone from her. "I was talking," she told her.

Courtney collapsed back on to her chair. "I really think you should check it. It's urgent, really urgent," she said as she made more faces at Liz.

"Sorry, I better look at this I suppose," Liz said to Reece and Ruth. She held up the phone and started reading. The room was once again in silence. Everyone watched as Liz's face as her eyes scanned across the text. Her eyebrows grew gradually closer together. "Chuff!" Liz said loudly and angrily when she finished reading the message from Lizzy. She took a second to compose herself.

She lowered the phone and smiled at Reece and Ruth. "Yes, I did get back from the shop quick. I don't hang about. I'm like a lightening bolt. Whoosh," she said, completely overdoing it, before smiling awkwardly.

"I hope it wasn't bad news," Reece told her as he fished for a response. Liz stared at him. "The message," he clarified.

"Nah, Courtney was exaggerating again," Liz said. Courtney scowled at her. "It was just work related," Liz explained.

"So soon, after you left?" Ruth asked.

"Yes, err, um," Liz glanced at the message again for a few seconds,"Ruth. They can't survive without me." Liz looked around the room and noticed Eric was looking lost with the conversation too. "Actually, Eric," Liz said, "I think you should read it too."

"Why would I want to read a message from your work?" he asked her. Liz held the phone in front of him. He glanced at the screen and saw Lizzy's name at the top of the message. "Oh, okay," he said as he took the phone from her. He read the message from Lizzy. "Ah, I see," he said as he handed the phone back.

"What is it dad?" Reece asked.

"Not important," Eric responded. Reece and Ruth weren't complete idiots and could see something strange was going on but neither commented on it. Instead they looked at each other with puzzled expressions.

"Cuppa?" Liz said before any further discussion was possible.

"Actually we had one just before we got to your shop," Reece said.

"You'll be ready for another," she said before they could answer. "Give me a hand Courtney," she said as she practically pulled her towards the kitchen. Liz quickly shut the living room door as she left.

"Me either, normally I take the school holidays but Anthony's work is flexible with him taking those. So I could change mine," she said as she opened up the company app on her phone.

"That doesn't help much if I'm not due mine for a while," Claire pointed out.

"Rank has it's privilege," Kelly said cryptically as she scanned her phone.

"What do you mean?" Claire asked

"I can't change the rotas but I can read everyone's," she explained, fully aware she was probably bending the company rules a little. Her eyes didn't move from her phone. "Not enough notice for this week, right," she said to herself. She looked up again. "Okay there are five people on holiday next week. We need two of them to swap with us."

"It's a good idea but that's kind of short notice," Claire told her.

"There's five of them, we only need two," she said optimistically. Claire didn't have her confidence. Kelly looked at the screen again and tapped it a few times. "Plan B then. Our finishing times line up a few times this week," she said optimistically, "I could give you a lift back here, we could have dinner together and then...," she trailed off knowing Molly was in the room.

This plan seemed more viable to Claire than the first plan, at least in the short term. "Stuff?" she asked, using there newly acquired code word for sex.

"I thought I could have one normal day." Liz groaned as she talked to Courtney in the kitchen. Although Liz was feeling emotional she was moderating her volume so the others didn't hear her. "It's not much to ask. Is it? It started so well too."

"This isn't Lizzy's fault," Courtney said.

"I'm not blaming her," Liz said defensively as she put some biscuits on a plate.

"Makes a change," Courtney said. Liz frowned in response. "Maybe it's a blessing in disguise, Liz." Courtney added.

"How so?" Liz asked, scarcely believing that was possible.

"You did say you would tell Reece about Lizzy," she reminded her.

"At the right time. When he's by himself." Liz told her as she opened another packet of biscuits.

"Might as well be now. If you tell him, he probably tell Ruth anyway."

"Too many people know already," she said feeling a little worried. She felt like she was losing control. "Do you know anything about her?" Liz asked.

"Who? Ruth? Not much, I think she's a journalist or something. Writes news articles online," Courtney said as she mimed typing on a keyboard. "Sounds quite interesting,"

"Oh, chuffing hell," Liz said as her head flopped down. "It will be all over the internet." In her mind, she pictured a huge headline including the words - bigamist, lesbian, fraud and clone.

Courtney rubbed Liz's shoulder in an attempt to comfort her. "I think you are worrying over nothing. It won't come to that," she reassured her.

Liz lifted her head. "I wish I had your confidence," she said. She looked straight at Courtney's face. "For now we keep it a secret. Nobody else is to know. Understand?"

Courtney nodded reluctantly.

"Nobody!" Liz repeated.

"Cool Mummy goes to work and Mummy stays in our house then Mummy goes to work and Cool Mummy stays at home but Cool Mummy doesn't live here, she lives in another house with her friend. Cool Mummy just visits here,"

"Mummy and Cool Mummy?" Ruth asked trying to understand the young boy's logic.

Ethan nodded. "Yes, two mummies," he said as held up two fingers. "They are magic."

Eric looked on transfixed and terrified as Ethan happily told his son's girlfriend the actual truth. Ruth looked back at Eric as Ethan spoke. Eric thought he'd better say something. "What wonderful imaginations they have at that age," he said calmly before smiling.

"He sounds like he really believes it too," Ruth said.

"People might get the wrong idea if he says things like that," Reece commented, being somewhat less than progressive in his thinking.

He turned to his younger brother. "Ethan, you have a mummy and a daddy."

Ethan nodded in agreement. "Two mummies and a daddy," he corrected him.

Reece groaned, he thought that sounded worse. Ruth gently poked him. "Don't be so judgemental, he can have as many mummies and daddies as he wants," she told him.

Ethan smiled in response, showing off his few missing baby teeth.

Bored with the conversation Ethan pushed himself out and back on the floor. He grabbed his tablet off the coffee table. Sitting in a corner, he once again became immersed in his games.

Reece shook his head and turned to Eric. "He's nearly six dad. Isn't he getting a bit too old for imaginary friends?" he asked.

Eric shrugged, honestly having no clue what to say. He was still coming to terms with Lizzy's existence himself. He was saved by Courtney opening the living room door. Liz was behind her carrying the large tea tray.

Ruth put her hand on Reece's lap while looking at Ethan. "I think it's kind of cute," she said.

"What's cute?" Liz asked as she walked in.

"Oh, Ethan has got an imaginary friend," Ruth explained.

"Has he?" Liz asked. This was the first she had heard of it.

"It's weird," Reece said, "An imaginary mother."

Luckily Liz had just about put the tray down when she felt a wave of shock pass through her. There was a loud thud as the tray hit the table. The teaspoons made a noisy clink and several biscuits slid off the plate,

"You all right, luv?" Eric asked as he spotted her distress.

"Yes, yes," Liz panted, she feared she might be starting to hyperventilate. She glanced at Courtney who opened her eyes wide as she made a very slow shrug, so the others wouldn't notice. Liz felt her pulse speed up. She hoped she wouldn't faint again, she was worried she wasn't completely over the concussion from the last time.

Reece went back to the previous conversation. "He calls you mummy," he said before he chuckled. "And the other one is the Cool Mummy," he told her as he chuckled again.

Liz's panic was immediately replaced with anger. She felt betrayed by her own child. "Oh, she is Cool Mummy, is she?" Liz asked slowly, the emotions were hard to hide.

Reece's smile disappeared. "You're not jealous of Ethan's imaginary friend are you Liz?" he asked.

Liz gave him a long stare. Gathering as much willpower as she could she turned her expression into a smile. "No, that would be silly," she said in the most pleasant voice she could muster. "He's a little rascal," she stated as she started pouring the tea.

Claire leaned over and gave Kelly a peck on the cheek.

"Good luck," Kelly told her, knowing what she was about to do.

"I'll need it," Claire said as she opened the car door. She stepped outside and closed it again.

Kelly watched as Claire walked back to the entrance to her flat. Claire turned back round to look at her and blew her a kiss. Kelly smiled as Claire walked inside.

"I hope it goes well," Kelly said to herself.

"What?" Molly asked from the back seat.

"Claire has to do some things she really doesn't want to do," Kelly said.

"Like going to school."

Kelly chuckled. "Hey it's a lot better than when I went," she told her daughter, "Yeah, I guess."

The car fell silent as Kelly saw a light get turned on upstairs. It wasn't dark, but Kelly knew it might be a different story in Claire's dingy flat.

"She's funny and tall," Molly said, "I like her."

"I like her too, sweetheart," Kelly said before she started the car's engine.

—

As soon as Claire got in she took off the dress. With some difficulty she also managed to take off the bra. She continued undressing until she was naked.

Looking down, she peeled off the tape trapping her penis. She rubbed it a few times as it resurfaced. "Hopefully we won't have to do that again any time soon, little guy," she said to it. Now Anthony was more comfortable with the way she really was, she didn't want to wear the dress without good reason.

She slipped on a pair of boxer shorts up over her hips. She removed her glasses and put them on the dressing table. After picking up her masculine pair of glasses, she put them on her face.

Now out of 'girlie' mode, Claire felt more relaxed. Feeling a little cold she put on a hoodie but didn't zip it up.

She grabbed her phone and lay down on her bed. It seemed small to Claire, compared to the one at Kelly's.

Hesitating a little she starting entering a phone number. She had to enter it manually because it wasn't in her phone's memory. It hadn't been in it for a long time.

She pressed the call button and anxiously waited. It started to ring. Several times Claire was tempted to hit the cancel button.

The ringing stopped. "Hello?" A man's voice said.

Claire hadn't expected that when she called her mum. She knew she shouldn't have been surprised, her mother was still relatively young, not much older than Kelly. Claire immediately cringed at that thought.

Her mother had went out with a few guys when Claire had been there but nothing serious had ever came out of it. Her mother was probably lonely now, Claire wasn't there. She knew she was getting ahead of herself, perhaps her mother had just moved house.

"Hello?" The man asked again.

Claire realised she'd been spending too much time thinking. "Err, I'm looking for Mrs Dixon. She still lives there right?" Claire asked.

The man chuckled. "Oh, yes, she still lives here. Who shall I say is calling?" he asked.

"It's," Claire said before hesitating, there was no turning back now, "her daughter."

There was no response for several seconds. "Really?" he asked, sounding very surprised.

"Yes, really."

"Oh, okay can you hang on a minute, don't go anywhere."

The phone went silent. Claire was sure she heard voices in the distance.

"Claire?" her mother's voice asked, almost like she couldn't believe it.

A single tear ran down Claire's face. "Yes, mum," she said.

"Is, Is something wrong?" she asked, her voice sounded a little croaky like she was getting emotional herself.

"No, no. Everything is fine, better than fine," Claire said in effort to stop her from worrying. "I just wanted to call you."

"Well, something must have happened because you've never called me before," her mother's voice became tinged with anger.

"Sorry, this was a bad idea–" Claire was about to hang up.

"No, wait," her mother pleaded, "I'm sorry, it's just..."

Kelly had warned Claire her mother would probably be upset to begin with her either way. "I get it," Claire said, "You're angry at me. You have a right to be. I haven't contacted you in a long time." In the past she would have just met her anger with her own.

"Yes, I'm bloody angry," her mother told her. She stopped talking and there was silence on her end.

Claire thought she heard the man's voice in the distance. "Mum?" Claire called out.

"I'm still here. I just needed a moment to calm down, I can't afford to be getting myself upset."

"Are you sick?"

"No, I'm not sick," she said. Claire didn't think her mother was lying but she knew there was something she wasn't telling her. She decided to let it go for now. "How's things at Wilko?" she asked.

"How do you know where I work?" Claire asked. Her mother rarely came into town and usually shopped in the out of town centres.

"Barry saw you," she said. Barry was Claire's cousin. "Said you still had short hair and looked like a guy,"

"Yeah, I should have guessed Barry would say something like that. I'm genderfluid mum!" Claire said trying not to get upset.

"Genderfluid, I don't even know what that means," her anger level was rising again.

"It means sometimes I feel like a man and sometimes I feel like a woman. Would you believe I've been wearing a dress for the last day?" she asked. Actually she'd been naked most of the last day but she didn't think that would help her explanation.

"A dress? Not like you, Okay I think I understand a bit better now," she said calmly.

"You're not going to argue with me about it?" Claire asked.

"Well, I think it's a bit strange and I can't say I like it much but it's not worth fighting over."

Claire's jaw dropped. "You used to argue with me about my lifestyle all the time," she said, astonished her mother was backing down.

"I wouldn't say argue. I might not have liked it and told you so then you'd fly off the handle and stop talking to me. I didn't agree with it but I did want to understand."

"Oh," Claire said, thinking back to their arguments. She wondered if her immature mind had misremembered all this time. Although Claire thought her mother was painting herself in a good light, she may have actually had a point. Maybe if Claire had explained her decisions she would have listened more.

"Besides, you're old enough to make your own choices now. You have been since you left," her mother added.

"So you got a bloke now?" Claire asked in an effort to find out more about the mystery man.

"Uh-huh, Charlie, he's the best thing that's happened to me in a long time," she said. Claire could tell she meant it.

"I'm glad to hear it."

"And what about you, have you got someone?" her mother asked.

"Sort of," she admitted vaguely. Kelly was happily married and she didn't want to be the source of gossip about her.

"Man, woman or genderfluid?" she asked slightly sarcastically.

Claire kept her emotions in check. "A woman," she said through gritted teeth, "Kelly."

"Good," she said before pausing, "Look, it's silly doing this over the phone. We should really meet up. There's a few things I need to tell you about. I want to do it in person. Maybe lunchtime one day this week."

"I don't know if that is such a good idea mum," Claire said, fearing a full on battle.

"I'll bring Charlie and you bring Kelly. That should keep things civil," she said.

"I'll have to ask Kelly first."

"Uh-huh. Any day is fine for me, send me a text. You still have my mobile number?"

"Somewhere," she said. Like the home number, she had long ago memorised it but removed it from her phone. "Is Charlie okay with alternative lifestyles?" she asked, he was an unknown factor and she worried it could be an issue.

"Oh, no worries there, he's very open-minded. He used to be married to a man," her mother told her without emotion.

"Wait, what?" Claire asked, scarcely believing her ears.

"Ha! You're not the only person in the world with an alternative lifestyle," she said, taking great pleasure in silencing Claire, "Text me about meeting up. Bye," she said rapidly before hanging up, ensuring she got the last word.

Claire stared at her phone in total astonishment.

Liz took her own cup and sat on the spare armchair. They all sat quietly drinking their tea when she spied Ethan grabbing a third biscuit. "Put that back, you've had two already," Liz told him firmly. She fully expected him to say 'Cool Mummy' would let him have it. She mentally noted to have a word with Lizzy about that. If I actually get through today, she thought to herself.

She was happy to see the frowning child return to his corner. She actually felt herself becoming a little more relaxed.

The room was too quiet for Liz's liking and she wanted to find out more about Ruth. She was the first girlfriend Reece had been with in a while since his breakup with his former fiancée Paige.

Liz turned to her. "So Ruth?" Liz asked. The young woman turned towards her. "Courtney tells me you're a journalist."

Ruth laughed, "I'd like to be. I got my degree a few years ago. I mostly do agency work, you know factories and stuff like that," she explained.

Liz felt a sense of relief. She suspected Courtney had got the wrong end of the stick. "So you don't write articles online?" she asked.

Ruth took a sip of her tea. "Oh, yeah I do that," she said. The relaxation Liz felt evaporated. "Piece work usually, here and there, you know." Liz nodded stiffly. "I'm always on the look out for my first big scoop though." She put her cup down on the coffee table.

"Big scoop?" Liz asked her vocal chords straining.

"My first big story," she said emphasising the word big by stretching her arms out. "It could make my career. I'd be set for life."

"What, err, what kind of story?" Liz asked, struggling to get the words out.

"I'm not fussed, anything really, the wonderful, the weird, something interesting," she said excitedly. Liz squeaked. Ruth's journalist instincts finally kicked in and her eyes narrowed. "Do you have something for me?" she inquired.

"No!" Liz said a bit too loudly. "My life is boring. I wouldn't know any interesting stories."

"Shame," Ruth said as she picked up her cup again. "I'm surprised."

"Why would you be surprised?" Courtney asked, curious about what Ruth meant.

"It's one of the stories I've been investigating here. Liz's shop has a bit of a reputation in the local paranormal circles," she said.

"You follow Rotherham's paranormal circles?" the surprised Courtney asked.

"Not normally," Ruth admitted. "I've done my research though, the street outside the shop is one of the most haunted in South Yorkshire."

"What a load of crap," Reece said, clearly stating his opinion.

Ruth huffed at him. "It's true, there have been seventeen reports of a mysterious couple who appear and disappear out of nowhere," she said putting a spooky lilt in her voice. "They go back to the nineteen forties, some people have speculated they are ghosts of people who died in the war."

The room fell silent before Courtney spoke. "Does sound a bit far-fetched," she said.

"There's photographic proof."

"Really?"

Ruth pulled out her phone. "There was a professional photographer taking photos of the Wilko building in 1972, he caught them on camera," she said as she looked at her phone. "Here it is."

She handed the phone to Courtney who looked at the high quality black and white photograph. She recognised the old building immediately. It was clearly being used as a supermarket back then. She could see lots of bystanders on the street. Amongst the miniskirts and bell bottoms was a pair that clearly stood out. They seemed to be dressed in more modern clothing. Courtney zoomed in on them and laughed.

Ruth leaned over. "What's so funny?" she asked.

"I don't think they had plastic carrier bags during the war." Courtney said. "You can see the guy holding one."

"Oh," Ruth said, she felt a little foolish for not noticing that before.

Courtney glanced back at the phone and found something else interesting. She tried zooming in again but she reached the limit on the phone as the photo started to get grainy. She squinted at the small screen. "Is that Carly?" she asked amazed at the resemblance.

"Who is Carly?" Ruth asked.

"A friend of Liz's. She's a beautician," Courtney said as she handed the phone to Liz. "What do you think?" She asked Liz.

Liz barely looked at the screen but she could tell it was clearly her. "Nah, nothing like her," Liz said dismissively as she handed the phone back to Ruth.

"I'd like to talk to her, maybe it's a relative," Ruth told them.

"She's got a flat in town. Liz knows the address," Courtney said.

Liz stood up and ignored both of them. "I better get these things back into the kitchen." She grabbed the teacup from Eric's hand before he'd even finished. In a hurry, she piled everything back on the tray before picking it up. "Door, Courtney," she said. Courtney opened it. "Come give me a hand." She instructed.

Courtney followed reluctantly, thinking Liz was acting rudely towards Ruth.

"And close it," Liz told her.

Courtney shut the door and walked through to the kitchen.

"What the chuffing hell are you playing at Courtney?" Liz asked the young woman.

"Nothing, I'm actually quite curious. I'm sure it is Carly. Ruth could show her the photo. She could do it today before they head home," Courtney said, thinking it was a good idea.

"And where would they meet exactly?" Liz asked.

"I don't know. Carly's flat I'd imagine," she said as it hit her like a ton of bricks. "Oh, shit!"

"Yes, oh shit indeed," Liz said slowly.

"Lizzy will be there."

"Probably," Liz said.

"I'm sorry, I didn't think." Courtney said, feeling genuinely remorseful. "It did look like Carly."

"Yes," Liz agreed without explaining.

"But that photo is nearly fifty years old, it must be her grandmother," Courtney guessed.

"You've got an over-active imagination. This is real life Courtney not TV. People aren't identical to their parents and grandparents," Liz said, pointing out a flaw in her logic.

"So who is it then?" Courtney asked.

"Carly from the future. The year 2030. Her and that bloke," Liz felt a little dizzy when she tried to remember his name,"she's with are travellers. They've been journeying back in time," she finally finished explaining. Courtney looked bemused. "They are dropping off magic items through history. They gave me the thing that made Lizzy."

"And you have the cheek to tell me that I have an over-active imagination," Courtney told her, sounding very sceptical.

"Well if you've got a better explanation for the photo. I'd love to hear it," Liz said, unhappy with the attitude she'd got off her stepdaughter.

"Okay, I can't think of one right now," Courtney told her, "The important thing is you have to tell Reece about Lizzy. Ethan already blabbed about it. If you keep denying it and he keeps doing it, they'll think he's deliberately lying. That's wrong." she said, feeling protective of her little brother.

Before Liz could answer, they heard the living room door open and close. Reece came walking through. "You forgot my cup," he said holding it up. He put it in the sink. Liz and Courtney watched him silently. He turned round to face them. "What's going on?" he asked earnestly.

"Nothing!" Liz said, while Courtney remained silent.

Reece looked between them both. "You might have fooled Ruth but you aren't fooling me. You two have been acting strange since we got here," he said before he paused for thought, "Dad too, now I think about it."

Liz put her hand on Reece's wrist. "Ruth mustn't go to Carly's," she told him, sounding desperate.

Reece took her hand and pulled it off his wrist. "Why not?"

"I can't say," Liz said.

Reece turned to Courtney. "Will you tell me?" Reece asked.

Courtney shifted her jaw from side to side a few times before taking a deep breath. "Oh, what the hell," she said, angry at Liz for making her do this, "Liz has a secret double and Carly is her lesbian lover. They live together in Carly's flat." she told Reece.

"Is that it?" he asked.

Courtney shook her head. "No that's not all of it, that's not even the best bit. Every so often the secret double pretends to be Liz and sleeps with dad," she added.

"That's enough! You're twisting everything." Liz told her. Her stress levels were through the roof.

Reece ignored Courtney and turned to Liz. "Maybe you can give me a sensible answer then," he suggested, "Like why you were acting weird with that old photo?" he asked.

She knew she had to tell him something and it seemed safer than talking about Lizzy. "It is Carly in the photo and she is a time traveller from the future," Liz said, putting her hands up, beyond caring any more.

"Okay fine, don't tell me the truth. If that's how it is," a clearly very angry Reece said as he turned back around. "Ruth!" he shouted.

Ruth peeked her head round the door as Reece walked down the hall. "What is it?" she asked.

"We're going," Reece said without turning round.

"Oh okay," she said, sensing Reece's mood. She quickly said goodbye to everyone including a disappointed, tearful Ethan.

Courtney leaned in close to her. "Carly's salon is called Beautylicious," she quickly confided. Ruth smiled in gratitude for the tip.

Reece stood at the front door waiting for her. Once Ruth had stepped through, he slammed it loudly.

Liz looked at her disappointed family gathered around her. "Well, I think that went well," she said.

Three pairs of eyes stared at her.

Now the computers were working again, Lizzy was responding to all the e-mails Emma had ignored over the previous few days. She was quite content being on her own in the office. She was a bit worried about Liz though. Lizzy hadn't heard anything from her since she sent her the message warning of Reece's imminent arrival. She hoped that was a good sign.

As she typed, there was a loud creaking noise. She paused for a moment to have a look around the office, nothing seemed amiss. She caught movement in her peripheral vision and noticed a pen rolling along the length of the desk. Lizzy watched as it fell off the end. It hit the hard floor with a clink.

14112.content:
It was a bright and early morning in the Rotherham town center as Caleb stepped out into the storefront to assume his duty for the day. Extending his arms up toward the clear sky to stretch, Caleb smiled in the radiant sunshine. It was a nice start to another typical day at Wilkos.

An old man, who was just getting out of his silver Toyota, tripped and fell on the asphalt. His accident prompted Caleb to come to him.

"Are you OK?" Caleb asked, as he steadied the senior as he got to his feet. Besides some minor cuts and bruises, he luckily didn't sustain other injuries.

"Yes, thank you, good sir," the old man replied. His voice sounded just as frail as his appearance.

With Caleb's help, the man hobbled his way back into his car. When Caleb wanted to ask if he need more attention to treat his wounds, the old man simply waved him off, stating that he had sustained worse in his life.

As the man settled back into his seat, he gestured to Caleb toward the vehicle interior, as if he wanted help with the seat.

Calen leaned in, expecting further instruction, when he felt a strong shock to the neck. As he felt his consciousness fade, Caleb's last glimpse was a wicked smile strung across the old man's face with a taser-like device in his hand.

When Caleb next woke, he felt.. light, as if all burden was lifted. In reality though, that was far from the truth as if he found his body to be translucent. In front of him, he saw the old man's collapsed body, lying flat across the car seat. If he was not unconscious, he might be dead. Of course, he was probably the least of Caleb's concern right now. "What happened...to me? Did I die?" Caleb could only ask himself as he tried to make sense his current situation. Frantically looking around, he thought he saw himself, fitted with the same uniform he wore, walking through the entrance to Wilkos before disappearing behind the automatic glass doors.

"Wait!" Caleb tried to chase his 'double' before feeling a strong force tethering him. Looking back, he saw his ethereal form attached to a chain of sorts linked to the car, the site of his own collapse.

With a limit to how far he could float, Caleb lacked many options to save himself. Additionally, the parking lot was relatively early, given the early time of day. Even if there were people, Caleb feared that they would not be able to hear his pleas, should his worst suspicions hold true.

But hope was not lost, as Caleb saw a car entering the lot. Actually, there were two cars, as they parked near the Wilkos storefront.

First, a silver Nissan parked, and out came Rashid, the Arab student employee at Wilkos. A little bit on the quiet side, but Caleb could tell that he was friendly and hardworking from the few conversations they had. Because he worked part time, his schedule was somewhat irregular. He seemed to be free from classes today, as Rashid rarely took morning shifts. Young and athletic, Rashid seemed to have just woken up, given his unkempt hair and disheveled clothes.

The other car was a red Mercedes, which Caleb quickly recognized as Gemma's car. The HR rep's arrival usually spelled doom for Caleb because of her numerous 'repetitive' trainings and her sharp tongue, worsening with her more regular visits. As expected, she was dressed to the nines in her professional outfit with nary an imperfection.

Both would walk into Caleb's zone of travel on their way to Wilkos. The question was who should Caleb try to contact for the best shot in understanding his situation.

14121.content:
Emma was currently hiding in the warehouse at Wilko. It was supposed to be her day off, but she snuck in to perform an experiment.

Emma had acquired an obsidian ring from the dark web. Supposedly it would allow the wearer to possess other people's bodies. Emma wasn't sure she believed this thing would actually work, but she was eager to try. Her husband had taken the kids away to their grandparents for the week, so now was the time to experiment.

Crouched behind a wooden crate, Emma bit her lip in anticipation. She decided that she would possess the next person to walk into the warehouse, regardless of who they were.

Emma could here footsteps approaching the warehouse. The assistant supervisor, Steven, had sent Kiran into the warehouse to grab a box of shampoo to restock some shelves.

As soon as Kiran walked in, Emma made sure to hide behind the crate, then placed the obsidian ring on her pinky finger. The ring seemed to adjust itself to fit exactly to her finger.

Before she knew it, Emma realised that she was now in the form of a ghost, her ghost form identical to her own body wearing the same clothes.

Emma wasted no time and made a beeline for Kiran, forcing her ghostly form into Kiran's diminutive body. Kiran's body convulsed for a few moments, almost like it was trying to fight back against Emma's forced entry, but it was no use. After only a few brief seconds, Emma was in full control and Kiran's mind was asleep.

Emma took in a deep breath with her new lungs.

"Mmm, I feel so young and full of energy! And these itty bitty titties are so pert!" Emma giggled in her new voice as she helped herself to a handful of Kiran's stolen B-cup tits.

As Emma continued to feel up her new body, she saw a foot out of the corner of her eye, sticking out from behind the crate that she was hiding behind only moments ago. The foot seemed to be wearing the same sneakers that she was wearing earlier when she was still in her own body.

Emma rushed over to the crate she was hiding behind earlier and was shocked to see her own lifeless body slumped against the crate.

"Oh shit!" Emma said panicking. She assumed that the ring would turn her into a ghost. What it actually did was vacate her soul from her body.

Emma quickly checked the pulse of her old body, before sighing in relief. Her body was still alive, still breathing. But she had to find somewhere to hide it. She wasn't ready to leave Kiran's body yet, she wasn't even sure she knew how to leave Kiran's body? That was a problem for later. Right now she needed to find a hiding place.

Emma looked around and spotted an empty box on the other side of the warehouse. It might just about fit her body inside.

Emma grabbed her former ankle and started to drag her former body across the warehouse floor.

"Damn why did I have to posses the smallest person in the store!" Emma grunted from her now 5'0" tall, 22 year old body as she dragged her former 47 year old, 5'4" body across the warehouse floor.

Emma sighed as she watched her body getting covered in dust and grime as she tried to be careful as she dragged her body.

Emma finally arrived at the empty box, but she didn't have time to rest. She could hear footsteps aporoaching. Maybe it was Steven wondering where Kiran was. Maybe someone else was looking for some stock.

Emma had to be quick, she grunted and strained as she lifted up the deadweight that was her former body and carelessly dumped it in the box with a thud. Maybe the box was too small after all. Emma panicked as she tried to cram her former limbs in the box.

Emma cringed as she forced her former body into the box, she somehow managed to force one of her former legs behind her head. She knew she'd have serious cramp when she eventually left Kiran's body and got back into her own. She managed to force another limb into the box, this time and arm, but there were still two more limbs to go and Emma could hear the footsteps getting louder.

Emma managed to finally force the remaining arm into the weak cardboard box that was obviously too small... just in time too as Steven walked in as just as Emma closed the flaps to the top of the box.
